meta.labor_cost_correction_note: Par Brink source PDF (and matching Hourly Sales And Labor.pdf) reported labor_cost=$11,674.10 / labor_percent=244.65% for this business date. Hourly breakdown showed absurd per-hour wage rates ($145-$324/hr) for hours 2PM-7PM only, vs normal ~$11-12/hr for the surrounding hours -- a Par Brink POS-side labor cost calculation error, not a parser bug (confirmed by re-parsing both Sales Summary.pdf and Hourly Sales And Labor.pdf independently -- both agree exactly on the bad figure). Corrected labor_cost/labor_percent below sourced from CrunchTime API cross-check (data/labor_today.json snapshot pulled 2026-07-17T10:50:19 for date=2026-07-16: labor_dollars=1095.87, labor_percent=22.9673%, actual_hours=97.43), which is in line with surrounding days (7/15: $907.68/19.74%, 7/17: $1,019.62/22.94%). labor_hours left as Par Brink's own 95.43 (internally consistent with its per-hour sum and the printed Sales Per Labor Hour figure -- not part of the anomaly). Raw uncorrected Par Brink figures preserved below for audit trail.
